heading: Acquisition of land; exclusion from Beaverhead National Forest; administration
label: 430uu–2 Acquisition of land; exclusion from Beaverhead National Forest; administration
notes: References in TextThe Act entitled “An Act to establish a National Park Service, and for other purposes”, approved (; ), referred to in subsec. (c), is , , known as the National Park Service Organic Act, which enacted sections 1, 2, 3, and 4 of this title and provisions set out as a note under , National Park Service and Related Programs. Sections 1 to 4 of the Act were repealed and restated as , Crimes and Criminal Procedure, and section 100101(a), chapter 1003, and sections 100751(a), 100752, 100753, and 102101 of Title 54 by , §§ 3, 4(a)(1), 7, , , 3260, 3272. children.label: a
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior may acquire by donation, purchase, exchange, or otherwise, lands and interests in lands within the area described in .section 430uu–1 of this title

children.label: b
children.text: Any lands described in that are a part of the Beaverhead National Forest on , are hereby excluded from the forest and added to the Big Hole National Battlefield.section 430uu–1 of this titleMay 17, 1963

children.label: c
children.text: Lands included in the Big Hole National Battlefield pursuant to sections 430uu to 430uu–4 of this title shall be administered in accordance with the provisions of the Act entitled “An Act to establish a National Park Service, and for other purposes”, approved (; ), as amended and supplemented.August 25, 191639 Stat. 53516 U.S.C. 1–31See References in Text note below.1
